description: This figure displays the grain boundary misorientation distribution of Fe-8%Al low-density ferritic steel after hot deformation, characterized via electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD). The horizontal axis represents misorientation angle (°), while the vertical axis shows the frequency of each misorientation value. A red vertical line at 15° distinguishes low angle boundaries (LAB, <15°) and high angle boundaries (HAB, ≥15°). Quantified results indicate LAB accounts for 85% of total grain boundaries, HAB accounts for 15%, and the average misorientation angle is 9.4°. The frequency declines sharply with increasing misorientation angle, with most values concentrated below 10°, which demonstrates the dominance of low-angle subgrain boundaries generated during dynamic recovery and continuous dynamic recrystallization (CDRX). This result validates the mechanism that subgrain boundaries gradually transform into high-angle boundaries during CDRX under appropriate hot deformation conditions.
